data.narrative:
Sateliot is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €70M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 3 private companies at identical stage and sector (Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network), with valuations ranging from $400M to $2.0B. Public comparables in Space & Aerospace span 5 tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 42.9x price-to-sales multiple, though this method cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures.

The peer-median approach is the sole applicable triangulation method, anchoring on the $1.3B median valuation across the three private comparables. This reflects the wide dispersion within the cohort: Cowboy Space trades at $2.0B while Hubble Network sits at $400M, underscoring heterogeneity in Space & Aerospace Series B valuations. The public comp universe establishes sector-level multiples but cannot calibrate to Sateliot without revenue disclosure.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.1B to $1.5B, centered on $1.3B. Key uncertainties include the limited peer set (n=3), revenue visibility relative to the high public sector multiples, and the wide $1.6B spread within comparable Series B companies. Positioning within this range would require clarity on growth trajectory, customer traction, and satellite deployment progress relative to peers.
